summary: Good‑quality matrix recording with standout China > Rider and horn‑enhanced Truckin > Eyes; tight band performance despite a short second set.
review: The show captures the 1973 Philadelphia run with a solid, tight Dead that rolls through memorable moments like a soaring China > Rider and an inspired horn‑driven Truckin > Eyes into Sugar Mag. Recording quality is mixed – the opening Aud mono segment is thin, but the soundboard feed takes over for most of the set and sounds clear and well‑balanced.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (6/8) rate the show 4–5 stars, praising musical highlights and noting good soundboard quality; only one low‑star review complains about the horns and early audience source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Opening portion is Aud mono with limited fidelity; the remainder switches to a clean soundboard feed that many reviewers call “great” or “crisp.” Overall listenable despite early limitations.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds nicely to the high‑energy China > Rider, while the second set is shorter than typical 1973 shows but remains tight and horn‑rich, delivering memorable jams like Nobody's Fault and Stronger Than Dirt.
